# services.gitlab-runner

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.gitlab-runner`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.gitlab-runner.checkInterval` | `signed integer` | Defines the interval length, in seconds, between new jobs check. The default value is 3; if set to 0 or lower, the default value will be used. See [runner documentation](https://docs.gitlab.com/runner/configuration/advanced-configuration.html#how-check_interval-works) for more information. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.concurrent` | `signed integer` | Limits how many jobs globally can be run concurrently. The most upper limit of jobs using all defined runners. 0 does not mean unlimited. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Configuration file for gitlab-runner. {option}`configFile` takes precedence over {option}`services`. {option}`checkInterval` and {option}`concurrent` will be ignored too. This option is deprecated, please use {option}`services` instead. You can use {option}`registrationConfigFile` and {option}`registrationFlags` for settings not covered by this module. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Gitlab Runner. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.extraPackages` | `list of package` | Extra packages to add to PATH for the gitlab-runner process. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.gracefulTermination` | `boolean` | Finish all remaining jobs before stopping. If not set gitlab-runner will stop immediatly without waiting for jobs to finish, which will lead to failed builds. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.gracefulTimeout` | `string` | Time to wait until a graceful shutdown is turned into a forceful one. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.package` | `package` | Gitlab Runner package to use. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.prometheusListenAddress` | `null or string` | Address (\<host>:\<port>) on which the Prometheus metrics HTTP server should be listening. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.sentryDSN` | `null or string` | Data Source Name for tracking of all system level errors to Sentry. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | GitLab Runner services. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.buildsDir` | `null or absolute path` | Absolute path to a directory where builds will be stored in context of selected executor (Locally, Docker, SSH). |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.cloneUrl` | `null or string` | Overwrite the URL for the GitLab instance. Used if the Runner can’t connect to GitLab on the URL GitLab exposes itself. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.debugTraceDisabled` | `boolean` | When set to true Runner will disable the possibility of using the `CI_DEBUG_TRACE` feature. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.dockerAllowedImages` | `list of string` | Whitelist allowed images. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.dockerAllowedServices` | `list of string` | Whitelist allowed services. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.dockerDisableCache` | `boolean` | Disable all container caching. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.dockerExtraHosts` | `list of string` | Add a custom host-to-IP mapping. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.dockerImage` | `null or string` | Docker image to be used. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.dockerPrivileged` | `boolean` | Give extended privileges to container. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.dockerVolumes` | `list of string` | Bind-mount a volume and create it if it doesn't exist prior to mounting. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.environmentVariables` | `attribute set of string` | Custom environment variables injected to build environment. For secrets you can use {option}`registrationConfigFile` with `RUNNER_ENV` variable set. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.executor` | `string` | Select executor, eg. shell, docker, etc. See [runner documentation](https://docs.gitlab.com/runner/executors/README.html) for more information. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.limit` | `signed integer` | Limit how many jobs can be handled concurrently by this service. 0 (default) simply means don't limit. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.maximumTimeout` | `signed integer` | What is the maximum timeout (in seconds) that will be set for job when using this Runner. 0 (default) simply means don't limit. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.postBuildScript` | `null or absolute path` | Runner-specific command script executed after code is pulled and just after build executes. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.preBuildScript` | `null or absolute path` | Runner-specific command script executed after code is pulled, just before build executes. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.preCloneScript` | `null or absolute path` | Runner-specific command script executed before code is pulled. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.protected` | `boolean` | When set to true Runner will only run on pipelines triggered on protected branches. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.registrationConfigFile` | `absolute path` | Absolute path to a file with environment variables used for gitlab-runner registration. A list of all supported environment variables can be found in `gitlab-runner register --help`. Ones that you probably want to set is `CI_SERVER_URL=<CI server URL>` `REGISTRATION_TOKEN=<registration secret>` |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.registrationFlags` | `list of string` | Extra command-line flags passed to `gitlab-runner register`. Execute `gitlab-runner register --help` for a list of supported flags. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.requestConcurrency` | `signed integer` | Limit number of concurrent requests for new jobs from GitLab. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.runUntagged` | `boolean` | Register to run untagged builds; defaults to `true` when {option}`tagList` is empty. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.services.<name>.tagList` | `list of string` | Tag list. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.sessionServer` | `submodule` | The session server allows the user to interact with jobs that the Runner is responsible for. A good example of this is the [interactive web terminal](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/ci/interactive_web_terminal/index.html). |
| `services.gitlab-runner.sessionServer.advertiseAddress` | `null or string` | The URL that the Runner will expose to GitLab to be used to access the session server. Fallbacks to {option}`listenAddress` if not defined. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.sessionServer.listenAddress` | `null or string` | An internal URL to be used for the session server. |
| `services.gitlab-runner.sessionServer.sessionTimeout` | `signed integer` | How long in seconds the session can stay active after the job completes (which will block the job from finishing). |
